cases.query: how do I stop severe bleeding

result.passage: For severe bleeding, apply firm direct pressure with clean cloth, gauze, or the cleanest available material. Keep pressure steady. Do not repeatedly lift the dressing to check the wound because that can restart bleeding.
result.title: Severe Bleeding

results.title: Shock Warning Signs

results.passage: For minor cuts and scrapes, first stop bleeding with gentle direct pressure. Wash your hands if possible, then rinse dirt from the wound with clean running water or the safest available water. Remove loose debris with clean tweezers if available.
results.title: Basic Wound Care

cases.query: what should I do for burns

cases.query: what are signs of dehydration

result.passage: Prevent dehydration by drinking small amounts regularly, resting during the hottest part of the day, and reducing unnecessary exertion. Dark urine, dizziness, dry mouth, confusion, and inability to sweat can be warning signs.

cases.query: how do I manage insulin and medicine during a disaster

result.passage: Prioritize medicines that prevent immediate harm, such as insulin, seizure medicine, heart medicine, asthma inhalers, blood pressure medicine, anticoagulants, and psychiatric medicines. Do not intentionally skip critical medicine to stretch supply unless a qualified clinician tells you to.
result.title: Medication Management During Outage

cases.query: how do I save phone battery

result.passage: To extend phone battery life, reduce screen brightness, enable low-power mode, turn off Bluetooth, Wi-Fi, mobile data, location, and background app refresh when not needed. Use airplane mode when communication is not currently required.

cases.query: is food in the fridge safe after a power outage

result.passage: During a power outage, keep refrigerator and freezer doors closed as much as possible. A closed refrigerator can usually keep food cold for several hours, and a full freezer stays cold longer than a half-empty freezer. Opening the door repeatedly lets cold air escape quickly.
result.title: Food Safety During Power Outage

results.passage: During a power outage, keep refrigerator and freezer doors closed as much as possible. A closed refrigerator can usually keep food cold for several hours, and a full freezer stays cold longer than a half-empty freezer. Opening the door repeatedly lets cold air escape quickly.
results.title: Food Safety During Power Outage

results.passage: Wash hands before handling food, after using the toilet, after touching trash or floodwater, and before caring for wounds. If running water is unavailable, use a small amount of safe water poured from a container. Soap and friction matter; do not dip dirty hands into stored drinking water.
results.title: Sanitation and Hygiene During Outage

results.passage: Some medicines need refrigeration. Keep them in the coolest safe place available, such as an insulated bag with cool packs, but avoid freezing unless the label says freezing is safe. If temperature-sensitive medicine may have been damaged, keep it labeled and ask a pharmacist or clinician as soon as possible.
results.title: Medication Management During Outage

cases.query: how do I keep hygiene when toilets do not flush

result.passage: If toilets do not flush, keep human waste away from living, cooking, and water storage areas. Use a lined bucket or container only if necessary, cover waste after each use, and store it where people, animals, and floodwater cannot spread it. Wash or sanitize hands afterward.
result.title: Sanitation and Hygiene During Outage

results.passage: A compass points toward magnetic north, not always true north. Keep it away from phones, speakers, magnets, vehicles, and metal tools while taking a bearing.
results.title: Compass Navigation

results.passage: If you need direction during a disaster, evacuation, or infrastructure failure and do not have a compass, use more than one method when possible. Treat improvised direction finding as approximate, then confirm with landmarks, maps, roads, stars, sunrise, sunset, or repeated checks.
results.title: Finding North Without a Compass

results.passage: Al navegar con mapa, considere la declinacion magnetica local si la conoce. Con poca visibilidad, avance despacio y use tramos cortos entre puntos identificables.
results.title: Navegacion con Brujula

cases.query: find north without a compass during evacuation

result.passage: Night method: in the northern hemisphere, find the Big Dipper if visible. The two outer stars of its bowl point toward Polaris, the North Star. Polaris sits roughly above true north. If clouds, buildings, smoke, or light pollution block the sky, do not rely on this method.
result.title: Finding North Without a Compass

results.passage: Night method: in the northern hemisphere, find the Big Dipper if visible. The two outer stars of its bowl point toward Polaris, the North Star. Polaris sits roughly above true north. If clouds, buildings, smoke, or light pollution block the sky, do not rely on this method.
results.title: Finding North Without a Compass

results.passage: A compass points toward magnetic north, not always true north. Keep it away from phones, speakers, magnets, vehicles, and metal tools while taking a bearing.
results.title: Compass Navigation

results.passage: If evacuation is ordered, leave early when you can. Roads, fuel, public transport, and visibility can get worse quickly.
results.title: Evacuation Bag

cases.query: I am lost and not sure whether to keep walking

result.passage: If you become lost, stop moving unless staying put is immediately unsafe. Panic walking wastes energy and can move you farther from known routes.
result.title: If You Are Lost

results.passage: If you become lost, stop moving unless staying put is immediately unsafe. Panic walking wastes energy and can move you farther from known routes.
results.title: If You Are Lost

results.passage: If you have ice, a cooler, or frozen water bottles, group high-risk foods together and keep them cold. Melted ice water can contaminate food if packaging leaks, so keep food sealed and drain dirty water away from eating surfaces.
results.title: Food Safety During Power Outage

results.passage: Si esta perdido, detengase, respire y evite caminar impulsivamente. Moverse sin plan puede alejarlo de rutas, refugio o rescatistas. Revise peligros inmediatos como clima, lesiones, agua, fuego o trafico.
results.title: Si Esta Perdido

cases.query: how can I signal rescuers

result.passage: Signals for rescuers should be simple, repeated, and easy to notice. Three repeated sounds, flashes, or marks are widely recognized as a distress pattern in many contexts.
result.title: Emergency Signaling

results.passage: Signals for rescuers should be simple, repeated, and easy to notice. Three repeated sounds, flashes, or marks are widely recognized as a distress pattern in many contexts.
results.title: Emergency Signaling

results.passage: Haga senales desde un lugar seguro. No suba estructuras inestables ni entre en areas peligrosas solo para mejorar la visibilidad.
results.title: Senales de Emergencia

results.passage: Avoid making travel decisions from one weak clue. Metal objects, electrical equipment, moving water, wind, slopes, and poor visibility can mislead improvised methods. If direction is uncertain and staying put is safe, conserve energy, mark your location, and signal for help.
results.title: Finding North Without a Compass

cases.query: three flashes whistle or marks to get rescue attention

result.passage: Signals for rescuers should be simple, repeated, and easy to notice. Three repeated sounds, flashes, or marks are widely recognized as a distress pattern in many contexts.
result.title: Emergency Signaling

results.passage: Signals for rescuers should be simple, repeated, and easy to notice. Three repeated sounds, flashes, or marks are widely recognized as a distress pattern in many contexts.
results.title: Emergency Signaling

results.passage: Use visible and audible signals when safe: bright cloth, reflective surfaces, whistle blasts, lights, or ground markings. Conserve battery and energy.
results.title: If You Are Lost

results.passage: If water may be contaminated, bring it to a rolling boil for at least one full minute. At elevations above 2,000 meters, boil for at least three minutes. Let the water cool in a covered, clean container.
results.title: Water Purification

cases.query: how can I use an emergency radio during an outage

result.passage: Keep radio volume low and use headphones when possible. Store spare batteries with the radio, protected from moisture and extreme heat.
result.title: Emergency Radio

results.passage: Keep radio volume low and use headphones when possible. Store spare batteries with the radio, protected from moisture and extreme heat.
results.title: Emergency Radio

results.passage: Mantenga el volumen bajo y use audifonos cuando sea posible. Guarde baterias de repuesto con la radio, protegidas de humedad y calor extremo.
results.title: Radio de Emergencia

results.passage: Make a simple household emergency plan before conditions get worse. Write down names, phone numbers, medical needs, meeting places, and out-of-area contacts on paper. Keep a copy near the exit and another with important supplies.
results.title: Household Emergency Plan

cases.query: como purificar agua en una emergencia

result.passage: Guarde agua potable en recipientes limpios, aptos para alimentos y con tapas ajustadas. Mantenga los recipientes cerrados, a la sombra y lejos de combustible, quimicos, basura y agua de inundacion.
result.title: Almacenamiento de Agua

results.passage: Guarde agua potable en recipientes limpios, aptos para alimentos y con tapas ajustadas. Mantenga los recipientes cerrados, a la sombra y lejos de combustible, quimicos, basura y agua de inundacion.
results.title: Almacenamiento de Agua

results.passage: Cuando el agua es limitada, primero separe el agua segura para beber del agua para lavar, limpiar o descargar el inodoro. Etiquete los recipientes con claridad. Proteja el agua potable de manos sucias, vasos abiertos y recipientes reutilizados que tuvieron quimicos o combustible.
results.title: Racionamiento de Agua

results.passage: Para purificar agua que puede estar contaminada, hiervala antes de beberla cuando tenga combustible seguro. Lleve el agua a hervor fuerte por al menos un minuto. En zonas de gran altura, hierva por al menos tres minutos. Deje enfriar el agua en un recipiente limpio y cubierto.
results.title: Agua segura durante un apagon

cases.query: agua sucia despues de un desastre y no se si hervirla

result.passage: Si tiene agua sucia despues de un desastre y no sabe si hervirla, trate primero el sedimento visible con una tela limpia y luego hierva cuando pueda hacerlo con combustible seguro. No use agua que huela a gasolina, solventes, pesticidas o aguas negras si existe otra opcion mas segura.
result.title: Agua segura durante un apagon

results.passage: Si tiene agua sucia despues de un desastre y no sabe si hervirla, trate primero el sedimento visible con una tela limpia y luego hierva cuando pueda hacerlo con combustible seguro. No use agua que huela a gasolina, solventes, pesticidas o aguas negras si existe otra opcion mas segura.
results.title: Agua segura durante un apagon

results.passage: No toque el interior de las tapas ni la boca del recipiente. Vierta el agua en vez de meter vasos dentro del recipiente. Si un recipiente se contamina, limpielo y desinfectelo antes de volver a usarlo.
results.title: Almacenamiento de Agua

results.passage: No racione el agua tan estrictamente que las personas se deshidraten. Reduzca la perdida de agua: descanse en sombra, evite esfuerzo innecesario, protejase del viento y el calor, y coma alimentos muy salados o secos solo cuando haya suficiente agua.
results.title: Racionamiento de Agua

cases.query: necesito pedir ayuda en espanol durante una emergencia

result.passage: En una emergencia, use mensajes cortos y claros para pedir ayuda en espanol. Diga donde esta, cuantas personas necesitan ayuda, que peligro existe y que recursos tiene.
result.title: Comunicacion de emergencia

results.passage: En una emergencia, use mensajes cortos y claros para pedir ayuda en espanol. Diga donde esta, cuantas personas necesitan ayuda, que peligro existe y que recursos tiene.
results.title: Comunicacion de emergencia

results.passage: Revise el plan cuando cambien las condiciones. Un plan seguro durante un apagon puede no ser seguro durante inundacion, incendio, humo quimico u ordenes de evacuacion. Siga instrucciones oficiales cuando esten disponibles.
results.title: Plan de Emergencia del Hogar

results.passage: Mantenga el volumen bajo y use audifonos cuando sea posible. Guarde baterias de repuesto con la radio, protegidas de humedad y calor extremo.
results.title: Radio de Emergencia
